messages.author: human
messages.text: is it me you are looking for

messages.author: ai
messages.text: Thanks! The three-layer architecture (Protocol, CLI, UI Adapters) is designed to keep each concern independent. Glad it reads well.

messages.author: ai
messages.text: Could you clarify what you'd like changed about the review file format? The JSON structure uses threads as first-class objects — is it the schema itself, the naming, or the overall approach you'd like revised?

messages.author: ai
messages.text: The four statuses (`open`, `pending`, `resolved`, `outdated`) cover the core ping-pong model. We discussed simplifying from 5 to 4 statuses earlier — dropping `addressed`/`discussed` in favor of the cleaner `open`/`pending` turn-based model. If you feel a status is missing for a specific workflow, happy to add it.

messages.author: ai
messages.text: Did you mean to type something else here? If this was a test comment, feel free to resolve it.

messages.author: ai
messages.text: This section describes the `:q` submit confirmation. Would you like a different confirmation flow, or is the concern about the keybinding choice itself?

messages.author: ai
messages.text: Great — the Claude Code integration loop is the key piece that closes the human-AI feedback cycle. The `/review-spectral` skill handles the full loop: invoke spectral → read results → process threads → re-invoke.
